_doc: Dated, cited, swappable rate table for the crossover-K cost model (spec §9). A skeptic must be able to audit every number here. Neither Modal nor (yet) AWS-for-g7e exposes a programmatic price for these, so they are constants with provenance. Update the date + source when you refresh them.
_asymmetry: K compares R_m for the card the script ASKED FOR against R_a for the card we SUBSTITUTED TO (decision #5). Do not collapse both to one card's rate.

modal._api: none — Modal publishes no programmatic pricing API; these are transcribed from the pricing page (per-second toggle).
modal._note: Base rates. Modal applies multipliers the model must account for: region-pinned = 1.5–1.75x base; non-preemptible (guaranteed, no eviction) = 3x base. Batch inference is typically preemptible-tolerant, so base is the fair default; the model exposes the multiplier so a skeptic can see the guaranteed-capacity comparison too.

aws._source: AWS Pricing API (get-products, AmazonEC2), us-west-2, Linux/Shared/Used, 2026-07-15.
aws._note: NOTE: the smallest g7e size is g7e.2xlarge (there is NO g7e.xlarge — the family starts at 2xlarge). g7e IS in the Pricing API and priced live.

g7e.2xlarge.source: live: aws pricing get-products, us-west-2, Linux/Shared/Used, 2026-07-15 (8 vCPU, 64 GiB, 1 GPU)

g6e.xlarge.source: live: aws pricing get-products, us-west-2, Linux/Shared/Used, 2026-07-15

g6.2xlarge.source: live: aws pricing get-products, us-east-1, Linux/Shared/Used, 2026-07-16 (8 vCPU, 32 GiB, 1 L4). The capacity-fallback instance the real N=1 run actually landed on.

buydown._note: Lower rungs Modal structurally cannot offer (spec §9). STATIC CONSTANTS for the spike — flagged as such, not measured.

_instance_gpu_count_doc: GPUs per instance size, so a per-GPU Modal rate compares to a whole-instance AWS rate correctly. Single-node g7e.2xlarge = 1 GPU for the spike.
